mnemonics.title: Reflexive - 'R' for 'Repeat'
mnemonics.content: For Reflexive relations, every element relates to itself. Think: 'I repeat myself' - each element appears in a self-relationship. Memorize: (a,a) is always in the relation for all a in the set.

mnemonics.title: Symmetric - 'S' for 'Same'
mnemonics.content: Symmetric means if a relates to b, then b relates to a. The 'S' stands for 'Same' - same relationship goes both ways. Think mirror reflection: a→b means b→a is identical.

mnemonics.title: Antisymmetric - 'A' for 'Alone'
mnemonics.content: Antisymmetric: if aRb AND bRa, then a MUST equal b. Only equal elements can go both ways - 'alone' on the双向 street. Otherwise, one-way only.

mnemonics.title: Transitive - 'T' for 'Travel Through'
mnemonics.content: Transitive means 'traveling through' an intermediate: if a→b and b→c, then a→c. Like a train: A to B, B to C, so A to C directly.

mnemonics.title: Irreflexive - 'I' for 'Impossible'
mnemonics.content: Irreflexive: it's 'impossible' for any element to relate to itself. (a,a) NEVER appears. The opposite of reflexive.

mnemonics.title: Asymmetric - 'A' for 'Absolutely One-Way'
mnemonics.content: Asymmetric: absolutely one-way. If a→b, then b→a is impossible. No element can relate back. Like a one-way street with no return.

acronyms.usage: These three properties define an EQUIVALENCE RELATION. Example: equality (=) on numbers.
These three properties define a PARTIAL ORDER (POSET). Example: ≤ on numbers.
These three properties define a STRICT PARTIAL ORDER. Example: < on numbers.

visualTips.visualization: In a relation matrix, mark the ENTIRE MAIN DIAGONAL (top-left to bottom-right). Every self-loop is present.
In a relation matrix, the matrix is a MIRROR IMAGE across the diagonal. If cell (i,j) is marked, then (j,i) is also marked.
In a relation matrix, only the diagonal can have symmetry. Off-diagonal symmetric pairs are forbidden (except on diagonal where i=j).
In a directed graph, if there's a PATH from a→b→c, then there must be a DIRECT ARROW from a→c. Fill in missing 'shortcuts'.
In a directed graph, NO LOOPS at any vertex. In a matrix, the main diagonal is completely EMPTY.
In a directed graph, if there's an arrow a→b, there can NEVER be an arrow b→a. No two-way streets allowed.

analogies.analogy: EVERYONE loves themselves (narcissism). In a group, each person is friends with themselves.
FRIENDSHIP: If Alice is friends with Bob, then Bob is friends with Alice. Mutual relationship.
FAMILY TREE - PARENT/CHILD: If Alice is parent of Bob, Bob cannot be parent of Alice. Only identical twins have 'symmetric' relation to themselves.
NUMBERS: If 3 < 5 and 5 < 7, then 3 < 7. Also: If Alice knows Bob and Bob knows Charlie, then Alice knows Charlie.
HATRED: Nobody hates themselves (hopefully). Zero self-relationships.
ONE-WAY STREET: If you can go from A to B, you cannot go from B to A. Strictly directional, no reverse.
